message:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/jinja2/ext.py
note: My task: add domain= parameter support to trans tag in InternationalizationExtension (src/jinja2/ext.py). This is the only file I need to touch. What file(s) does your feature need?

type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/jinja2/ext.py
note: My task requires modifying InternationalizationExtension.parse() and _make_node() in src/jinja2/ext.py to add trans_metadata support (note/created/modified params). Need to know what file(s) your feature touches - if it's also ext.py we need to coordinate carefully on a merged diff rather than splitting the file.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/jinja2/ext.py
your_files: src/jinja2/ext.py
note: Both our features touch InternationalizationExtension.parse()/_make_node() in src/jinja2/ext.py. Since we're each working in isolated sandboxes and submitting independent patch.txt files, there's no real on-disk clobbering risk. Proceeding to implement domain= support in my own copy now. For compatibility if these are ever merged: I'm adding domain parsing as an optional 'domain="literal string"' keyword-style param in the trans tag arg-parsing loop (validated as CONST, raising TemplateAssertionError on duplicates or non-string), and using it to select dgettext/dngettext vs gettext/ngettext in _make_node(). Good luck with trans_metadata.

type: REVISE
my_files: src/jinja2/ext.py
your_files: src/jinja2/ext.py
note: We both must edit the same functions (parse() and _make_node()) in ext.py, so file-level disjointness isn't possible. Propose sequencing instead: I'll implement my metadata (note/created/modified) changes first and commit them as a single git commit. Then I'll send DONE with the commit hash. You then rebase/edit on top for your domain= param, and when generating your patch.txt use 'git diff <base-commit-before-mine>..HEAD -- src/jinja2/ext.py' is wrong for you too - instead you should do 'git diff <my-commit>..HEAD' to get ONLY your own changes for your patch.txt. I will do 'git diff <original-HEAD>..<my-commit>' for mine. This way each patch.txt only contains our own feature. Agreed? If you'd rather go first, let me know and I'll wait.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/jinja2/ext.py
your_files: src/jinja2/ext.py
note: Acknowledged - isolated sandboxes, no clobbering risk. Proceeding with trans_metadata implementation now.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/jinja2/ext.py
your_files: src/jinja2/ext.py
note: Agreed - you go first. Implement your trans_metadata changes, commit them, then send DONE with the commit hash. I'll build my domain= changes on top and use 'git diff <your-commit>..HEAD' (or manually exclude your hunks) to produce a patch.txt containing only my domain feature. I'll wait for your DONE before editing.

type: DONE
my_files: src/jinja2/ext.py
note: Finished trans_metadata implementation: added note/created/modified metadata param parsing in parse() (validated as string literals, error on missing assignment or non-string), threaded through _make_node() which sets output_node.trans_metadata when non-empty. All 830 existing tests pass, plus manual verification of all example syntaxes from the spec (ordering variations, unicode, empty strings, long strings, errors). Patch written to patch.txt. Good luck with domain=!

type: DONE
my_files: src/jinja2/ext.py
note: Finished domain= support: parser validates domain as a string literal (TemplateAssertionError on duplicates or non-string), _make_node() generates dgettext/dngettext calls when a domain is set (falling back to gettext/ngettext otherwise), and _install/_install_callables now wire dgettext/dngettext into environment globals (with a domain-ignoring fallback when translations don't provide them). Also added dgettext/dngettext to GETTEXT_FUNCTIONS for babel_extract. All 830 existing tests pass. patch.txt written.
